mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0 percent, tight labor markets compress wage growth margins and signal peak cyclical demand, leading to mean-reverting equity corrections over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or threshold crossings show no predictive power for subsequent 60-day returns (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in real economic activity, which compresses corporate revenue expectations and drives equity sell-offs over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint decline signal fails to predict negative forward returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or partial correlation between INDPRO and HOUST drops below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During active monetary tightening cycles, quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent demonstrates earnings resilience that outweighs higher discount rates, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative in more than 55% of instances where GDP growth exceeds 2.0% during tightening periods, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.04 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in retail and food services spending above 3.0 percent signals accelerating consumer demand momentum, which translates into higher corporate revenue expectations and drives equity gains over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in > 50% of triggered months, or rolling 1-year Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When non-farm payrolls growth exceeds 150,000 while the federal funds rate remains below 1.0 percent, the combination of robust hiring and accommodative financing conditions maximizes corporate profit margins, driving strong equity outperformance over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform the baseline market return in more than 60% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost inflation and supports equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth shows no statistically significant correlation with subsequent 60-day equity returns (p > 0.05), or forward returns are flat across decile portfolios sorted by INDPRO momentum.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 2.5 percent year-over-year, persistent inflation erodes real discount rates and triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, leading to equity valuation compression over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in > 60% of instances where CPI exceeds the 2.5% threshold, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in a 5-year walk-forward test.
